title: Security Function Verification — Forwarder Config Change Auditing

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 SI-6 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 SI-6 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.38: Security Function Verification — Forwarder Config Change Auditing.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.38' running on index=_audit object paths under inputs/props, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=_audit earliest=-24h object="*/inputs.conf" OR object="*/props.conf"
| stats values(action) as actions, values(user) as users by object, _time
| search NOT user IN ("splunk_system_user","deployment_service")
description: Detects unauthorized changes to inputs and props that affect log integrity checks for SI-6 security function verification concepts at the logging layer.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Integrate with change tickets; (2) alert on non-service accounts; (3) snapshot approved configs in git; (4) quarterly drift review with CM-3. Indexes required: `index=_audit`.
visualization: Timeline (config changes), Table (who changed what). Key fields: `actions`, `object`, `users`.
knownFalsePositives: Security or system signals can spike during maintenance, vendor tests, and lab cutovers — check scope, asset criticality, and the scan schedule before you call it a failure.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_audit` object paths under `inputs`/`props`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Integrate with change tickets; (2) alert on non-service accounts; (3) snapshot approved configs in git; (4) quarterly drift review with CM-3.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit earliest=-24h object="*/inputs.conf" OR object="*/props.conf"
| stats values(action) as actions, values(user) as users by object, _time
| search NOT user IN ("splunk_system_user","deployment_service")
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Function Verification — Forwarder Config Change Auditing (SI-6)** — Detects unauthorized changes to inputs and props that affect log integrity checks for SI-6 security function verification concepts at the logging layer.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_audit` object paths under `inputs`/`props`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by object, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (config changes), Table (who changed what)
grandmaExplanation: We detects unauthorized changes to inputs and props that affect log integrity checks for SI-6 security function verification concepts at the logging layer. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
